channel.title: Checked Exceptions on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts
channel.description: Recent content in Checked Exceptions on Bazlur's Stream of Thoughts

item.title: How to Handle Checked Exceptions With Lambda Expression
item.description: Lambda expressions are all about how we write code. It’s about how we can write more concise, smaller, and less boilerplate code. However, this aforementioned statement may not seem to be true in the case of exceptions in Java. In Java, we can only handle exceptions through the try-catch block, and this hasn’t changed for the lambda expression. Let’s say we’re going to develop a simple web crawler. The crawler will take a list of URLs in a string as an argument and save the content of the text file in a text file.
